matches.match_rationale: The core pain is unreconciled discrepancies between two systems — tuition posted with no matching student record, or enrolled students with no revenue entry. Anomaly detection directly targets this: given paired finance/SIS records, it flags rows that deviate from expected patterns (e.g., enrolled-but-no-tuition, tuition-but-no-enrollment). This replaces the quarterly manual reconciliation with continuous automated flagging. Education operators already have the structured data in both systems; no new data infrastructure is needed.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI reconciliation engine that ingests nightly exports from the finance system and SIS, computes expected pairings, and surfaces mismatches as a prioritized exception queue — replacing the quarterly spreadsheet exercise with a daily or weekly automated report.

matches.match_rationale: The finance system and SIS use different schemas, identifiers, and timing conventions — tuition entries reference billing IDs while the SIS uses student/enrollment IDs, and revenue recognition timestamps differ from enrollment dates. AI-assisted schema mapping and normalization can create a unified canonical record that links the two systems without a full ERP integration. This is the prerequisite layer that enables reconciliation, and it is squarely in scope: semi-structured data from two systems needs reshaping to a common schema.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A lightweight ETL layer using AI-assisted field mapping that normalizes and joins finance and SIS exports into a single reconciliation dataset, handling ID translation, date alignment, and amount normalization — delivered as a scheduled pipeline or on-demand report.

matches.match_rationale: Beyond flagging mismatches, the operator needs to understand the category of each discrepancy — timing lag, data entry error, bad debt misclassification, or legitimate revenue/enrollment gap. A structured classification model trained on historical reconciliation outcomes can automatically label each exception row (e.g., 'revenue-recognition-lag', 'student-record-missing', 'bad-debt-misclassified'), enabling the finance team to triage and resolve exceptions by type rather than investigating each one from scratch.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A classifier that categorises each flagged reconciliation exception into a predefined taxonomy of discrepancy types, so the finance team can bulk-resolve common patterns (e.g., all timing-lag entries) and focus manual effort on genuinely unusual cases.

matches.match_rationale: The reconciliation workflow has multiple sequential steps: extract data from both systems, normalize fields, match records, flag exceptions, categorise them, update bad-debt status, and generate a summary report. This is a textbook multi-step business workflow with branching logic (matched vs. unmatched, lag vs. error vs. genuine gap). A task automation agent can orchestrate the full pipeline end-to-end — replacing a quarterly manual process with a continuous automated one that only surfaces items requiring human judgement.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An end-to-end reconciliation agent that runs on a nightly or weekly schedule: pulls exports from both systems, runs matching and anomaly detection, classifies exceptions, auto-resolves known patterns (e.g., clears delinquent flags for re-enrolled students), and emails a summary exception report to the finance team.

matches.match_rationale: Government funding claims and board reporting require narrative explanations of enrollment-revenue variances. Finance teams currently write these manually and must reconcile the numbers before writing. Once the reconciliation data is clean, an LLM can auto-draft the variance narrative — explaining why revenue recognition lags enrollment at each campus, summarising bad debt aging by cohort, and flagging funding claim discrepancies in plain language for sign-off by finance leadership.
matches.opportunity_sketch: Auto-generated variance commentary attached to each reconciliation report: a short narrative per campus that explains the delta between SIS enrollment and finance revenue for the period, suitable for board packs or government funding submissions.
